title: Claude Code Skills Pack: Team Standards Bootstrapping — 5 Skills for Communicating Coding Conventions to New Hires
description: For engineering leads and DevEx teams, this pack infers coding conventions from existing code, generates a CONTRIBUTING.md from scratch, documents naming patterns and folder structure rationale, produces a linting and tooling setup guide, and creates a decision log template capturing why key architectural choices were made so new team members understand not just what the rules are but why they exist.
gumroad_description:
ONBOARDING TAKES THREE WEEKS BECAUSE YOUR CODING STANDARDS LIVE IN SOMEONE'S HEAD.

This pack changes that. Five ready-to-use skills that let Claude extract your actual conventions, write your CONTRIBUTING.md, document why your folder structure exists, configure linting from your codebase, and build a decision log so people understand the reasoning behind the rules—not just the rules themselves.

WHO THIS IS FOR

— Engineering leads managing 5+ person teams without documented standards
— DevEx engineers tasked with scaling onboarding processes
— Teams hiring fast and tired of repeating the same explanations

WHAT'S INSIDE

— Inference skill: Analyzes existing code, surfaces hidden conventions
— Contributing guide generator: Creates CONTRIBUTING.md from scratch
— Pattern documentation: Names, folders, architectural choices explained
— Tooling setup: Linting and formatter configuration guide
— Decision log template: Captures the why behind key choices

Drop into your skills/ folder, run the quick-start, watch your new hires stop asking "but why?"

Download and use it today.

Discover more tools like this at mini-on-ai.com
